description: Retrying a non-idempotent write after a timeout, duplicating the effect because the first request had already succeeded.
body: Failure mode: a client retries a create/charge on timeout without an idempotency key, but the original request actually completed server-side, so the retry produces a second record or charge. Detection signals: duplicate resources or double-charges correlated with network timeouts; retries on mutating calls with no idempotency key; support reports of duplicates. Do not retry unkeyed mutations. Safer fallback: attach idempotency keys to writes, retry only idempotent operations with backoff, and test a timeout-then-success path to confirm exactly one effect.
